rubrics.title: Generic correctness
rubrics.description: Passes when the answer is non-empty and does not start with a hedge phrase — the floor rubric for free-form prompts where the operator hasn't picked a specialized rubric yet. Deterministic and fast.

rubrics.title: Patent claim validity
rubrics.description: Patent-strategist canonical rubric — looks for at least one of the claim-validity anchor terms (anticipation / obviousness / written description / enablement / 35 U.S.C. § 102 / § 103 / § 112). One of the three default rubrics so the patent vertical's compare runs produce comparable scores out of the box.

rubrics.title: MCQ letter (A/B/C/D)
rubrics.description: Cyber-bench canonical rubric — expects the final answer to contain a standalone 'A', 'B', 'C', or 'D' letter (whitespace- or punctuation-bounded), matching the multiple-choice format the cyber vertical bench uses. Per `feedback_keep_scorer_local_until_reuse`, this scorer was the first to ship beyond plain substring; promoted from the cyber preflight after the second-vertical-reuse gate.

stages.label: Scout
stages.headline: 30B-A3B leads 7/8 · base not locked
stages.detail: raw floors via visible Cortex preflight: Nano 9B /no_think 1/8 · Qwen2.5-7B 5/8 (comparison) · Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B /no_think + format exemplar 7/8 — §14 ≥7/8 pre-SFT bar met without training
stages.gate_consequence: Locks the base model; a wrong base (NC-license / unsupported llama.cpp-arch / oversize weights) sinks the whole pipeline.

stages.label: Corpus
stages.headline: 181 public-safe sources · recall@5 0.9885
stages.gate_consequence: Confirms the synth fits the weekly cap before a multi-session run; holding stops the spend.

stages.label: SFT-init
stages.headline: deferred — 7/8 floor met without training
stages.gate_consequence: SFT-init must beat base on held-out or the warm-start adds nothing; holding blocks the RLVR loop.

stages.label: Smoke
stages.headline: —
stages.gate_consequence: The behavioral smoke gates trusting the checkpoint; holding sends it back to corpus coverage.

stages.label: RLVR
stages.headline: not armed
stages.gate_consequence: Only a held-out-winning step promotes; holding keeps the prior checkpoint resident.

stages.label: Publish
stages.headline: routing probe + live recall + 8/8 receipt first
stages.detail: §13.F route bakeoff (T1 local → governed escalation) → §14 gates → publish or rejection receipt; no public Advisor positioning until then
stages.gate_consequence: Publish only on a held-out win; holding keeps the artifact unpublished.

queries.query: Why did the Hermes brain bakeoff pick a 30B MoE over NIM?

hits.text: The bakeoff pinned the Hermes brain to Qwen3-30B-A3B MoE at Q4_K_M on llama.cpp: 8/8 harness tasks against 6/8 for the NIM lane, at roughly 3.5x the tokens per second.
One serving lane at a time inside the 128 GB unified envelope — the harness points at whichever lane is resident, and the cockpit's discovery probe reports what is actually serving.
Routing sends local-shaped work to the resident lane and escalates to OpenRouter frontier models only when local confidence drops below the threshold.

queries.query: What recall did naive RAG get on the Second Brain eval?

hits.text: Naive top-k retrieval over the 313-chunk pgvector index lands recall@k 0.409 on the QA eval set — the floor the rerank and fusion experiments are measured against.
The QA eval harness scores recall@k and slug-recall@k per run and persists each run, so the trend over re-indexes is inspectable instead of anecdotal.
Fusing dense retrieval with a reranking pass lifts slug-recall toward 0.73 — the biggest single jump in the retrieval-path series.

queries.query: How much GPU memory does a LoRA fine-tune need on the Spark?

hits.text: Rule-of-thumb arithmetic: weights + optimizer states + activations, then check it against the 128 GB unified envelope — a rank-16 LoRA on an 8B model fits with tens of gigabytes to spare.
A 600-row QA LoRA on the Spark trains in minutes, not hours — the bottleneck is corpus quality, not compute.
Data-prep decisions (dedup, format, BOS/EOS) move the eval more than hyperparameters do; the gates are cheap to run before any multi-hour train.
